zh.lifeSummary: 當Millicent de Stafford於大約1157年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾斯塔福德時，她的父親Robert de Stafford20歲，母親Avice21歲。她和Hervey Bagot II至少有2個兒子。她去世於1224年1月25日在英國斯塔福爾史東享年68歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾史東。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Millicent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Melisende, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from amal ‘labour’ + swinth ‘strength’. This was the name of a daughter of Charlemagne. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Millicent de Stafford was born about 1157, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Stafford, was 20 and her mother, Avice, was 21. She had at least 2 sons with Hervey Bagot II. She died on 25 January 1224, in Stone, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Stone, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Millicent de Stafford est née environ 1157, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Stafford, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Avice, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Hervey Bagot II. Elle est décédée le 25 janvier 1224, à Stone, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Stone, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Sie hatte mit Hervey Bagot II mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 25. Januar 1224 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Stone, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Stone, Staffordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Millicent de Staffordが約1157年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで生まれた時，父Robert de Staffordは20歳，母Aviceは21歳であった。彼女はHervey Bagot IIとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1224年1月25日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストーンで68歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアストーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Millicent de Stafford(이)가 대략 1157년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Stafford(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avice(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Hervey Bagot II(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1224년 1월 25일 영국 스태퍼드실르 스톤에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스톤에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Millicent de Stafford родилась около 1157 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Stafford было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Avice, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Hervey Bagot II. Она умерла 25 января 1224 г. в местности стоун, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности стоун, стаффордшир, Англия.
